title: hostapi.load_host()'s except-and-fall-back-to-Claude-defaults path has zero test coverage anywhere in the suite
evidence: hostapi.load_host() (core/pysrc/hostapi.py:176-191, vendored byte-identically into both plugins) has a bare `except Exception: return Host()` branch that is the load-bearing fallback for the whole dual-host seam — every one of the ~19 `sys.exit(run(hostapi.load_host()) or 0)` entry points (grep across core/pysrc/*.py) depends on it choosing the RIGHT host or failing loud. Grepped the entire repo for `load_host` call sites AND for any test invoking it directly or indirectly with a broken/missing `_host.py`: none exists. `.github/scripts/test_codex_adapter.py` (read in full, 666 lines) always loads `_host.py` directly by file path via its own `codex_host()`/`claude_host()` helpers (lines 50-55) — it never goes through `hostapi.load_host()`'s try/except, and no fixture ever points load_host() at a directory with a syntactically-broken or absent `_host.py` to observe the fallback. `plugins/ca/hooks/tests/` was also checked (grep for load_host/hostapi/_host.py) with no hits. This is the exact silent-degrade path already flagged as a live bug by architecture-004/reliability-003 (dup'd in triage.jsonl) — the coverage gap is why it shipped unverified.
impact: The single riskiest branch in the whole M1/M2 seam (a broken ca-codex install silently degrading every enforcement entry to Claude-host semantics, per architecture-004/reliability-003) can regress or be 'fixed' incorrectly with no test to catch it — CI would stay green through either outcome.
recommendation: Add a unit test that points hostapi.load_host() (via monkeypatching __file__'s directory or a temp hooks dir) at (a) a missing _host.py -> asserts fallback to Host() with name=='claude', and (b) a syntactically broken _host.py -> asserts the same fallback is reached without raising. This directly enables verifying whatever remediation architecture-004/reliability-003 land on.
acceptance_criteria: A test exists that exercises hostapi.load_host()'s except branch (not a direct _host.py file-path load) against both a missing and a broken _host.py
The test asserts the observable fallback behavior (host.name, host.has_statusline, etc.), not just 'no exception raised'
